ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana’s sharp price drop, which has returned to 2023 levels after breaking below $70 amid panic selling. ETF outflows across major crypto assets, including SOL, have intensified, with only Hyperliquid’s HYPE ETF category seeing net inflows.
ctxTalking:
• Solana’s price decline and its implications for holders and protocols
• Large-scale ETF redemptions affecting SOL, BTC, ETH, and XRP
• Hyperliquid’s HYPE token overtaking SOL in price, though not in market cap
ctxMatters: Solana’s price action and ETF outflows may impact liquidity and sentiment for both users and builders. The introduction of native subscriptions and allowances on Solana could drive new use cases, even as market volatility persists. Ongoing support for onchain derivatives signals continued technical development despite price pressure.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Intense ETF outflows and Solana’s new onchain payment primitives are shaping both market and developer focus.
Noise: Short-term price comparisons between HYPE and SOL without broader context.
ctxGlossary:
ETF — Exchange-traded fund, a tradable investment fund holding crypto assets
Onchain Perps — Perpetual futures contracts built directly on a blockchain

readMore.title: Solana hits 2023 lows amid panic selling
BTC, ETH, SOL and XRP ETFs bleed $4.4 billion over 13 sessions, only HYPE in green
Hyperliquid’s HYPE overtakes Solana in price as SOL falls to lowest levels since 2023
